description_long: This is a modern high-rise apartment building with two main towers connected by a central section, featuring a mix of red brick and gray concrete facades. The structure is rectangular with clean, straight lines and a flat roof, typical of contemporary urban residential architecture. The building has numerous evenly spaced windows arranged in vertical columns, providing ample natural light to the interior units. The ground level includes a main entrance area and several smaller attached structures, likely for utility or service access. The exterior is primarily composed of brick and concrete, with some metal and glass elements visible in the window frames and doors. The overall appearance is functional and urban, designed to accommodate a large number of residents in a dense city environment.
description: A modern, multi-story apartment building with red brick and gray concrete facades, featuring two main towers, flat roofs, and rows of evenly spaced windows.
description_view_0: This view shows the front facade with the main entrance, red brick on the right, and a central section with gray and white panels.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the two parallel towers, both with large vertical window arrays and minimal visible brickwork.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the back of the building, with more gray and white paneling and a few attached utility structures.
description_view_3: This view shows the sides of the towers, one with a solid gray wall and the other with a solid red brick wall, and the attached ground-level structures.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are arranged in vertical columns with consistent spacing, creating a symmetrical and orderly grid pattern across all facades.
